claims.claim: WCAG 2.1 Level AA requires normal text contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1
WCAG 2.1 Level AA requires large text contrast ratio of at least 3:1
Israeli Standard ת"י 5568 governs digital accessibility requirements for websites in Israel
claims.source_title: Israeli Standards Institution (מכון התקנים הישראלי) - ת"י 5568

claims.claim: Israeli accessibility law (חוק שוויון זכויות לאנשים עם מוגבלות) requires an accessibility declaration page (הצהרת נגישות) on websites
CSS filter property creates a new stacking context, which causes position:fixed children to be positioned relative to the filtered ancestor instead of the viewport
claims.source_title: MDN Web Docs - CSS filter (stacking context behavior)

claims.claim: The html element should have lang and dir attributes set (e.g., lang="he" dir="rtl" for Hebrew) for accessibility and correct RTL rendering
claims.source_title: W3C WCAG 2.1 - Success Criterion 3.1.1 Language of Page

claims.claim: All interactive elements must be reachable by keyboard (Tab key) per WCAG 2.1 AA Success Criterion 2.1.1
Decorative images must have empty alt text (alt="") and aria-hidden="true" per WCAG 2.1
claims.source_title: W3C WCAG 2.1 - Success Criterion 1.1.1 Non-text Content

claims.claim: localStorage is available in all modern browsers and can be used to persist user accessibility preferences across sessions
